response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable full-stack and backend engineer with a broad polyglot background, but he does not meet the core mandatory requirements for this role. His C++ exposure amounts to roughly six months at National Instruments plus a bachelor thesis project — far short of the 5–10 years required. His primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang, and there is no demonstrated experience in capital markets, fixed income, investment banking, or related financial domains. SQL appears in his skills list but with no specifics around SQL Server or Oracle, and there is no indication of Windows-platform development. A single stint at RIDE Capital touched investment tooling but was Node/TypeScript-based and does not constitute capital-markets domain expertise.
strengths: Strong communication skills evidenced by public talks, multi-day workshops, and open-source project leadership
Demonstrated experience with CI/CD pipelines, DevOps tooling, and software-craftsmanship practices (TDD, trunk-based development)
Java background and polyglot experience across many languages shows adaptability and fast ramp-up potential
concerns: C++ experience is minimal (~6 months professional + thesis) versus the mandatory 5–10 years on Windows platform
Zero capital markets, fixed income, or investment banking domain knowledge beyond a brief Node.js engagement at a fintech startup
No evidence of SQL Server or Oracle proficiency, Windows-platform development, or any of the desirable skills (Summit, Kafka/MQ, Python, STL)
